answer: Argon2id with 64 MB memory, 3 iterations, and parallelism 4.
Refresh tokens expire in 7 days and are rotated on every use.
The Notification Service runs on port 9200.
PostgreSQL 15.4 is running now, upgraded from 14.9.
API Gateway throttle limits are 5000 burst and 2000 steady.
Argon2id was chosen over bcrypt for better side-channel resistance.
Drizzle is used instead of Prisma for zero runtime dependencies and SQL-like TypeScript patterns.
Both Portfolio and Notification services validate JWTs via JWKS (RS256 public keys), following the all-services-auth model.
Rate limiting is done at ALB because it blocks DDoS traffic before requests reach containers, which app-level limiting cannot do.

The chain is API Gateway schema validation -> Lambda authorizer JWT validation via JWKS with claims injection -> VPC Link to ALB rate limiting -> Portfolio Service JWT/JWKS validation plus RBAC role-hierarchy middleware before rebalance.

If JWKS goes down, token issuance in Auth continues (private key is local), but downstream auth validation fails in gateway/service validators after cache grace. A bounded LRU cache (1000 entries, 5-minute TTL) provides temporary grace.
Real-time portfolio alerting is planned for Q3 and depends on both Notification Service and a Gateway WebSocket API; it is not active yet.
Yes. The UTC+13/+14 token-expiry bug was resolved by using Unix timestamps instead of Date objects.

Use the existing webhook system: register enterprise endpoint via POST /api/v1/notifications/webhooks/register, emit auth events from Auth into Notification Service, and deliver with exponential backoff (5 attempts over 24 hours).
100 simultaneous connections (20 per instance across 5 Auth instances).
The 20-connection pool limits DB concurrency per instance, but it does not cap Celery process memory; large tax-lot recalcs can still grow memory unbounded during processing.
No. Fargate Spot is only for batch workloads; Auth is always-on and interruption risk is unacceptable.
The stateless JWT/JWKS doctrine (distributed token verification without shared session state) prohibits adding an in-memory session cache.
30-day backups define how far back restores are possible, while DR targets define recovery guarantees (RPO 1h, RTO 4h). They are complementary.
The upgrade required DB connections to use sslmode=verify-full with the RDS global-bundle.pem CA bundle.
tax_lots columns: id, account_id, security_id, acquisition_date, cost_basis, quantity, adjusted, created_at.
With MFA enabled, after password verification the login flow requires TOTP or a recovery code before tokens are issued.
10 recovery codes are generated; each is single-use and permanently consumed when used.
Webhook retries use exponential backoff with up to 5 attempts within 24 hours.

Credential/security posture: at rest, passwords are Argon2id (64MB/3iter/4par), PII is encrypted with AES-256-GCM per-tenant keys, and secrets rotate every 90 days; in transit, service traffic uses Envoy mTLS and PostgreSQL uses TLS with sslmode=verify-full plus global-bundle.pem.

Login lifecycle with MFA: client request enters API Gateway (schema validation), passes through VPC Link to ALB per-IP rate controls, reaches Auth handlePostLogin, password is verified with Argon2id, MFA challenge is completed (TOTP or recovery code), then RS256 access token (15 min) and refresh token (7 days, rotate-on-use) are issued.

A new Billing Service should follow platform patterns and constraints: validate JWTs via JWKS, integrate through Gateway/VPC Link/ALB limits, use RFC7807 errors with traceId, structured JSON logging with request IDs, real-Postgres testcontainers (no DB mocks), mTLS and verify-full DB TLS, 90-day secret rotation, GitHub Actions + Claude review + blue-green ECS deploy, and use Spot only if it is batch.

At 1500 req/min from one IP, API Gateway limits are not the first bottleneck (below 2000 steady), but ALB enforces 1000/min/IP so excess traffic is throttled before containers; only allowed traffic reaches Auth.

JWT signing-key dependencies: Auth Service uses the private key to issue RS256 tokens and publish JWKS; dependent validators using the JWKS public key are Gateway Lambda authorizer and all auth-enforcing services, including Portfolio and Notification.

Deployment pipeline: commit/PR runs GitHub Actions (lint -> typecheck -> test -> build -> push ECR image -> deploy ECS), Claude Code review is required before merge, then blue-green rollout switches ALB targets for zero downtime on ECS Fargate (Spot reserved for batch jobs only).
